title: Question banks #4-#6 across >= 2 subjects
context: M2 requires at least 6 banks adopted cumulatively across at least 2 subjects to demonstrate the pipeline generalizes and that throughput is improving vs. the M1 baseline. Banks #4-#6 must span at least 2 distinct subject areas and use advanced item types where applicable. Any regulated-domain subject matter encountered must be escalated to high risk tier with expert sign-off and 'not advice' framing, or excluded. Authoring effort is logged per bank to measure median improvement vs. M1.
objective: Author and deliver three additional question banks (banks #4-#6) spanning at least 2 subjects, each passing all gates and adopted by a beneficiary, with effort logged.
acceptanceCriteria: Three banks authored and delivered, spanning at least 2 distinct subject areas (e.g. language arts, history, computer science, earth science — not 3 math banks).
Each source passed gate-003; coverage >= 90% of stated objectives; machine gate green; all human sign-offs recorded.
Each bank adopted with adoption evidence artifact committed.
Any regulated-domain subject matter (health, legal, safety, finance) escalated to high risk tier with expert sign-off and 'educational, not advice' framing — or excluded if no expert available.
Per-bank authoring + review effort logged; median effort compared to M0/M1 baseline.
output: Three complete, adopted question banks across >= 2 subjects in canonical JSON + QTI 3.0 + Moodle exports, with gate artifacts, sign-offs, adoption evidence, and effort logs committed.
